text: I bought this to use with the two Twin XL adjustable beds we bought ([[ASIN:B074QWYM22 LUCID L300 Adjustable Bed Base-5 Minute Assembly-Dual USB Charging Stations Head and Foot Incline-Wireless Remote Control-Upholstered-Ergonomic, Twin XL, Charcoal]] - good news is the beds do fit, very (very) snugly. As other reviewers have mentioned, using this as just a frame requires removing the slat rails on the sides, and just omitting the slats and center supports altogether. The look of the headboard and frame is nice, though putting it together, you see up-close that the materials used are pretty cheap.  The two main reasons I am giving this just 2 stars - and they are important reasons! - are: #1 - the holes are drilled in the wrong places on the legs to the headboard. You cannot install the cross bar AND the sides to the frame - the holes are simply in the wrong places to do both. Of course we chose to install the sides to the frame, since we're not actually placing any weight on this headboard and frame. But if we were, I would be livid, since the center supports rely on being screwed to the crossbar to support the slats, and ultimately, a bed. I mean, this would be completely USELESS as it was sent to me for use as a bed. Reason #2 - nearly every hinge where I needed to connect pieces was totally bent.  I needed to use a clamp and a lot of muscle so I could straighten the hinges and screw them flush to the other part of the frame. Assembly was SUCH a frustrating experience because it was just poorly made. Shame on the manufacturer.

Silver foil cupcake "papers"... these are super handy and pretty too. They are regular cupcake size. Fit into regular muffin pan size. I use with savory baked goods that have a little more oil. No soaking or leaking or weakened paper. Perfect.

Listen, frying food is NOT a healthy choice, but as a treat? I think it's ok - I wanted something simple and easy to use - this Fry Daddy is truly that.  Just plug it in, let it heat for 15 minutes and it's ready. For the price I don't think it can be beat. It's not a "pro" model but works for 1 or 2 people well.  It keeps the oil at the right heat - I have tried a couple different foods and NOTHING was greasy (which would mean the oil wasn't hot enough). I just emptied and cleaned it after a couple of frys and it was easy to clean.  I used canola oil - and filled to the "full" line and I could not tell that any oil was missing - so that was also good.  I am looking forward to using this on those rare times we "fry" food - it's just fun and easy.  A couple of notes - I have a good silicone splatter guard - I recommend getting one - just really controls the splatter that naturally happens, especially if you're frying frozen foods (with water/ice crystals).  Also note that you cannot put the plastic lid back on the fryer until it has completely cooled.  Be careful where you place the fryer to use as it does get hot on the bottom and be mindful of what may become grease splattered as you are using it to avoid tragedy.  I like it.  Happy to use it.  Having said that - I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

I purchased these rechargable salt and pepper shakers because they came with a charging pad and LED lights. I have 2 rechargable shakers now that I must plug into a USB cord every few weeks - but no light. The problem with these is that the light is BLUE.  It's almost worse than NO light because the blue light is so blue you can't see where or what you are grinding!  The charge pad is handy, but you'll be constantly cleaning it because your shakers sit on the charger base - at the bottoms where the salt and pepper exit.  Also, you must press the button to grind - my current shakers turn on when you flip them - there is a button but no one uses it.  Additionally, the reservoirs for the peppercorns and salt crystals are VERY small.  Eh.  Minus 2 stars for the horrible blue light and another star dock for the way they are designed with the grinder opening on the charger base.  I am searching for a better set, at the very least with a white light.  I cannot recommend this set.  Not sure what I will do with them, probably donate them.  Honestly, one of the most disappointing purchase I have made in a long time.  Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. All of my reviews reflect my honest, personal experience with the reviewed item - your experience may be different. I am not influenced by any outside source. I receive/accept NO free products or discounts that are not available to all shoppers- ever. For some reason our shopper ranks are no longer visible - so, to give you a little more info about me, I am a top 50 reviewer (#30 the highest rank achieved). Those numbers used to fluctuate over time - up and down but I noticed that they stopped updating regularly - perhaps to phase them out. It's a shame because it did help you see who has been around the longest and who is a trustworthy reviewer.  I've been doing reviews for over 25 years with Amazon - over 6,000 reviews posted, those reviews have been viewed well over 50,000 times, including well over 24,000 likes. Bottom line, I pay for all my stuff, just like you do.

I am a tea connoisseur that loves loose leaf teas to be brewed correctly and at the right temperature. Unless one brews only black or pu-erh tea or herbal tisanes, then a brewer that brews at different temperatures is required in order to extract the correct flavor profile of that tea. This is where this Breville BTM500 Smart Tea Infuser is supposed to shine. After having tried this brewer, it does brew the tea correctly but it is so cumbersome to use and there are so many steps involved that it is not worth the price, in my opinion.  Before I go into the PROs and CONS of this particular teapot, I want to explain a little bit about the tools I use now to brew tea.  My favorite pot is a [[ASIN:B003LNOPSG Breville One-Touch Tea Maker]]. I originally purchased one of those at $250 and then purchased a second one for $199.99 because I felt I could not live without it if the first one broke. It is is the only tea brewer I know of that does a phenomenal job of brewing tea that is to be enjoyed to the last drip and brews with the least hassle. However, if I only want one cup of tea, I use the much simpler [[ASIN:B00DHLAC66 Frigidaire Professional Programmable Water Kettle]] to heat water to the correct temperature for one cup of tea.  I also occasionally use a tea kettle to just boil water to brew one cup of black tea with a tea infuser. When I want more than one cup of black tea, I use the [[ASIN:B007UEEJVK Nesco TM-1 Teamaker, 1-Liter]]  which acts like a coffee pot but for tea and only brews with boiling water.  Although this Smart Tea Infuser does produce a high quality brew, there is just not enough good about it compared to the bad.  WHAT I LOVE: - It brews a good cup of tea of varying kinds at the appropriate temperatures.  - Water can be boiled in this.  - There is a cord storage underneath the base.  - This is an attractive tea maker that looks great on my counter top.  WHAT I DISLIKE (in order of my biggest hate): - Brewing the tea requires several steps.  First the kettle needs to be filled with the desired amount of water. Second, the lid rim and top need to be inserted. Third, the tea type / temperature need to be selected. Then the unit needs to be turned on (a.k.a. started). Fourth, once the unit beeps (which takes several long minutes), the tea basket has to be inserted with the correct amount of tea. Fifth, the steeping time has to be set and then the START TIMER button pressed to start steeping. However, there is no light on that button to indicate it was even pressed. Once the cycle is complete, the tea maker is supposed to beep three times. I was unable to get it to beep. This means I now have to time how long I am steeping it for with a separate timer or sit and watch until the other lights on the pot go out.  Now the tea can be enjoyed but it is important to remove the tea basket first so it does not over steep. After the tea is poured, I have to follow more steps to keep it warm. I have to return the tea kettle to power base and press and hold the TEMP / KEEP WARM button for two seconds. This will keep the tea warm for 30 minutes unless I remove the kettle, after which I have to reset.  - The long brew basket makes it difficult to remove the spent leaves from the basket.  - The unit does not remember the last settings chosen after a power off.  - Custom programming is not possible as there is only a set selection of temperatures.  - There is no auto start option. This means one cannot wake up to a freshly brewed cup of tea. For the price of this unit, I would have expected this.  - The current cost of $149 is way too expensive for the cumbersome features of this brewer. I recommend spending $50 more to buy the [[ASIN:B003LNOPSG Breville One-Touch Tea Maker]]. Perhaps this is Breville's way of getting people to buy their one touch by making a substandard tea infuser.  - There is no secondary strainer at the spout of the carafe to catch any wayward leaves from escaping into my cup; although, this is unlikely given the tight "weave" of the brew basket.  - The base (obviously) and the carafe itself (that needs special care of wiping down or rinsing gently inside only) cannot be washed. This is the same with their one-touch tea maker.  MORE DISAPPOINTMENTS - The quick start guide and instruction manual contradict each other. The quick start guide says to leave the tea basket in when cleaning for first use; the instruction manual says to remove it. The quick start guides forgets to tell you about selecting the steep time and has you just starting the timer.  - The instruction manual contains erroneous tea information. Most tea is oxidized (exposed to air) and not fermented (converted via use of microbes). Although some use those words interchangeably, they do not mean the same thing and there is a lot of misinformation out in the tea world about this.  In summary, this is definitely not worth the purchase. There are entirely too many steps required to achieve the perfect brew. The tea basket is too long and narrow which makes it difficult to empty. In addition, the unit does not beep three times when the steeping time is over as the manual says. If you just love different loose leaf teas and want to experience the various subtleties in the liquor of these different brews, then save for the [[ASIN:B003LNOPSG Breville One-Touch Tea Maker]] because that tea maker is worth it and this one does not even come close.

UPDATE below:  Original review - I have longed to try one of these of many different brands.  After research and reading dozens and dozens of reviews, I landed on the Meater Plus.  I opened it up and found it to be in a beautiful wooden box which is actually it's charging station.  Comes with a AA battery for charging.  I downloaded the app.  I am 60 - so not a tech wizard.  I was able to sync with app very easily.  It did take a few minutes (maybe updating as well).  I just used it to cook a beef roast and it worked perfectly.  One thing I would note -  I was going for a medium rare - and "upped" the suggested temp of 135 to 140 because I was nervous... the Meater Plus counts the resting time in the finished temp!  So, I ended up with a little to the medium than to the medium rare.  I am going to trust the Meater Plus next time.  It notifies you of when to take your food out and then reminds you when the resting is done.  You can follow the progress on the app on your phone.  Has a lovely dial that shows where you are in the cooking progress.  I am very, very impressed.  I love it.  I am eager to try it in my all natural turkey for thanksgiving!  It's just a real handy tool with a modern twist.  It's a little bit of an investment, but I think it's worth it.  Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. UPDATED review  4/1/2021.... The more I use this thermometer the more I love it.  I have learned to "trust" the resting time - I have read other reviews that report overcooked food and I wonder if they might not be trusting the process.  However, could also be faulty items.  I am grateful that my thermometer has worked perfectly each time and I could not be happier.  It has taken so much stress out of my cooking life.  I won't roast without it.  Or cook a thick steak without it.  Pretty much anything you need temp on.  I am not compensated in ANY way for my reviews and I am sharing my honest experience and opinions. UPDATE 2022;  Therms will NOT stay connected to device.  NO tech support.  Takes WEEKS to get a reply via email and then it is truly worthless.  I am so disappointed because they (I have two pluses and a block) for about 4 or 5 months and then just will NOT stay linked.  SUPER frustrating both in lack of support they 'say' is there and then that they lasted just a few months. NOT a cheap lesson either.  I invested over $450.00 at this point.  I will sadly say I can no longer recommend these in any way.

Before using this ,I washed both mats that come with this with kitchen soap and a  brush. I used my gas BBQ, only turned on two of the burners, till the temp was about 325 to  350 degrees. Before puting the fish on the mat, I squeezed lemon on the mat, and sprinkled seasoning on. Put two different kinds of wild fillet fish on. Threw the package out, so don't remember what kind it was.  When I went back to check after 6 mins, the thicker white fish had allot of this foaming stuff coming out of it!! But was not ready to turn over., Neither was the thinner fish... Using the mat, it took longer for the fish to cook, although from reading other reviews, I was afraid to turn my BBQ to high.  When I finally turned the thicker fish over, it still continued to have this foaming stuff coming out of the fish. Didnt look like anytype of fish fat to me! Btw I have cooked that fish on a skillet inside the house on my gas stove before, and Never has it had this foamy stuff that kept coming out of it!  As far as washing this mat, for me it was a Pain. I now had dinner dishes in tne sink, and the mat had all this black mess stuck to it that I didnt want in my dishwasher. I had to lay it on my granite counter, and scrub it with kitchen soap, and a kitchen bush on both sides. It took some scrubing to get that stuff off.  I'm only going to give this a try again,using vegetables ..  For me I could have used a ceramic non stick skille for the fish, and it would have cooked faster, and Much easier, and faster  to wash. That said, it was 105 degrees in Ca. Today, but with the air going in the house, and a shorter amout of time To cook this in my house with the stove hood fan on, it would have worked  out  better for me.or just use my George formen grill.  If I get a different result I'll up date..

My first impression of this vacuum was that it was a four-star product.  However after a full evaluation, in my opinion this is a three-star vacuum for non-pet owners and those without allergies. Although this Bissell has a lot of PROS, it is a two-star vacuum for my purposes because I have pets and allergies to dust.  PROS: - Easy and intuitive assembly without reading manual (requires long Phillips screwdriver to tighten screw in handle which is a little awkward to tighten in the long hole) - Incredible suction - All tools fit snugly into their holders on the vacuum - The Model 58F8-3 comes with a combination dusting brush/upholstery tool, a crevice tool, extension wand, and TurboBrush - Retractable cord - No need to purchase bags - Has a bright headlight - The internal filter is washable; all except for the HEPA Media Filter which has to be replaced - Vacuum components are easy to disassemble without tools except for the headlight and drive belt replacement - 25-ft power cord (according to the product description online; but this is not mentioned in the manual) - Height adjustment knobs and other controls such as on/off switch and handle release pedal as easy to operate and locate - Easy to empty dirt container and remove filter cup - Has clean filter indicator and clean carpet sensor - Vacuum has a three-year limited warranty  CONS: - Vacuum was difficult to maneuver due to incredible suction - Vacuum is very loud - If the hose falls out of the hose wand base during vacuuming, a lot of dust escapes through the hole filing the house in a cloud - Hair from pets clogged the filter cup even before the filter cup was full rendering the turbo brush in operable until everything was cleaned out - Filter indicator light didn't change to red even though I had to clean out the filter cup - The clean carpet sensors did not appear reliable to me.  Many times it would show a steady green to indicate the carpet was clean, then it would turn to red when I went over the same carpet again and again; yet the carpet was clean. - Emptying the filter cup and cleaning the filter was VERY MESSY - Instructions don't give any recommendationos on when you should wash or replace filters - I don't understand the meaning of a HEPA filter when so much dust escapes the vacuum if the hose falls out of the wand base or when emptying the filter cup - The floor brush continues to rotate when you use the vacuum attachments which could wear out your carpet or mark up your floor while you are using the tools with the hose. - I would have liked a cord that was about 2-3 feet longer  My arm hurt after using this vacuum because no matter how I changed the height adjustment knob, the Bissell required a lot force to push around. I was actually sweating after I vacuumed my rug. Also, the pet hair I picked up got caught in the upper filter area requiring a cup cleaning and filter shaking before the filter cup was full. In addition, the filter emptying process (although very, very easy) was incredibly messy. By the time I was done, I had to re-vacuum the area where I emptied the filter cup. In addition, there was a cloud of dust around my house similar to the type of dust kicked up by a Shop Vac. Of course, I used a carpet powder on my rug which only exacerbated the problem. I also didn't like how the floor brush on the upright continues to rotate while I was using the wand tools. To me this was unnessary and could result in excess wear on my carpet or scratch my floors.  In summary, this vacuum does pick up a lot of dirt and is easily assembled, which is excellent.  Its interesting features are average.  However, I can't recommend this vacuum for someone who has pets or has allergies to dust for the above reasons.  Instead, I recommend buying a vacuum with a bag that isn't so loud or so messy to clean.

This is a great scale. I live on a farm where often we set up street side selling booths where I can sell mostly corn, cucumbers, carrots and strawberries. Normally a lot of what we do is portioned based on the bags we use other than the actual weight. Close your eyes. No, wait, open them, I need you to keep reading or this won't work. So, keep your eyes open, but kind of act like they should be closed. Better. Now, imagine yourself stepping out of the shower. It smells amazing. (You just went to LUSH, so this was quite possibly a holy experience here.) You feel great. You grab your towel. It is perfectly dried, still smells a little like your fabric softener but is that perfect towel feel. Everything is perfect. Then you step on a wet cat! Wait. What? You look down, hoping your cat isn't really under your feet. Nope. Just this rug. This cat fur rug. That looked so super chic when you placed it in front of the shower. It looked great with my coral honeycomb pattern shower curtain. A perfect postmodern mesh. But wet cat feel just ruined the shower experience. You try to figure out how to get off the wet cat rug. You figure you'll just finish drying off, since it is a bathroom rug and will certainly at least absorb the water rolling off your body. You'd be wrong, again. Wet cat is surprisingly unabsorbent. So after your shower, you have to dry the floor, since the wet cat proved worthless at his job. But at least he looks snazzy. No? No. He looks like a wet cat laying in front of your shower. A mangled wet dumpster cat. This is bad. Maybe when it dries it will be better? A little. But not the chic look you had before. Now your cat is dry, but has some odd cowlicks he didn't have before. Who thought faux fur would be a good idea in a bathroom? And why did I think it'd be a good idea? Did no one stop and say, "Hey, this might feel awful when wet and look like a wet cat?" I mean, I didn't. But I don't get paid to make cat rugs.

It's bought and placed it in 2 entry points in mid April when the ants scouts for food. I'm near the Boston area and only have pavement ants that are coming from a crawl space into my bathroom. I've read that this has the diffeo types food in one, carbs, protein, fats depending on what the colony needs at any particular time. I've mostly used Terro but they drown themselves in it constantly. Go away for 2 days and come back for more drowning and then they just walk around it. So after they disappear for 2 days, I placed a drop, afew ants smell it, back away from it and look for something else. I think they can smell the poison. Some might eat it for 2 bites and walk away. The rest will just walk away from it uninterested. I guess I'll go back to the Terro for the slow drowning and wasteful product for the drowning bodies. Sometimes I can't tell if they are trying to recover the dead ones and drown themselves during. No other option. No point in placing anything in the crawl space if they aren't eating this to give to the queen.

While some bad reviews have said it doesn't cook very hot or takes a very long time to reach a hot temperature, we've had the opposite experience. It cooks extremely hot, and the low setting makes soups boil. The outside of the crock pot gets so hot you can't touch it or risk really burning yourself (I say this because I saw a question & answer up above about it not getting too hot on the outside). It's a safety hazard for how hot it cooks!  1/9/2014: On the off chance that it was simply operator error ruining our dinners (like accidentally putting it on high where low should have been used instead), I gave it one last chance to pay extra attention. I had a 3-pound roast ready to go and put it on for four hours on LOW while I finished my day at work (which, theoretically, should take 8-9 to cook; 7 hours at the least). It was overcooked so badly by the time we got home that it's hard as a rock. You have to cut it harder than you do any overcooked steak. It must run so hot that it probably cooked the roast in an hour or hour and a half, tops. What's the point? I might as well cook my meat in the oven instead of trying to be efficient while at work. I'm getting rid of this piece of garbage. I've had enough of costly ruined dinners thanks to this thing.

I originally really liked this two bottle pump set when I bought them in May of 2020, and even bought a 2nd set in July of 2020.  Initially, all 4 pumped well with nice thick foam.  BUT by December of 2020 three of the four bottles would pump only a thin watery output with very large bubbles rather than foam.  To have a "refillable" pump fail in such a short time drops my recommendation to only a single star.  The pump should either be redesigned or manufactured with different materials to insure a reasonable life of pumping.  With the current pump, I WOULD NOT recommend any one purchase this two bottle pump set.

I am in a nursing home, and I had it sitting on a table at the end of my bed. I reached for it with my grabber tool, to pull it towards me, and the side literally just popped off. I started to do a return, but it said it had to be returned in original packaging, which of course I didn't keep. Or I could get an exchange, but I what if that falls apart too? I noticed that beside the tiny, insignificant nails, there is a trail of wood glue that is about the width of a human hair. I think if they had used bigger nails or more wood glue, it would have stayed together. I'm just going to try and hot glue it back together. If you live at a higher elevation like me (5k Colorado) just know that after the buzzer beeps you might wanna leave the hard boiled eggs inside the steamed cooker for another minute or two if you want the egg cooked all the way through. I also add a bit more water than the fill line says, but again that’s bc of my elevation & it evaporates quickly.  It’s not any quicker than I used to make them, but it’s a lot less water. I’m disabled & have a TBI rn so the auto-cutoff was a no-brained for me. I bought the blue bc it was cheapest.  My service 🐶  🐶 love hard boiled eggs too so we are enjoying this new addition to our kitchen.  My only regret is that I did not buy it sooner & that Amazon never has the color I want for the cheapest price, but I can live with the bb blue.  We have used it 3x & so far it’s working okay. Oh- don’t place it under upper cabinets as it has a vent hole & it will vent & it will make the cabinets sweat.  Give it some room to breathe imo.  I keep a pair of tongs handy to unload the eggs & dunk them in my ice bath. Yes. You end up using water again, but I have house plants I’ll use it on anyways so it not just wasted water.  If it holds up & doesn’t break down it will truly be one of my top 10 if not top 5 amazon lifetime purchases.  It’s that good.  Nothing is worse than watching eggs boil. Lol.  I start this up at the same time I start my tea brewing and go about feeding the wildlife in my backyard.  It’s just a very peaceful start to my day. I know ppl have said stuff about the buzzer, but honestly I happen to be hearing impaired in both ears & my service dogs are trained to hear for me. I like that it’s loud enough that if my pups are outside & I am in my living room or just outside on my patio with them & my screen door is open then they & often I can still hear it.  I wish appliances had volume options on their alarms, but until then I am very happy with this one.  I am just extremely happy with this purchase. It’s been a long time since I could say that. Lol.

This OXO GOOD GRIPS SOAP DISPENSING SPONGE HOLDER is one of the few OXO products that I don’t like.  The tall (3½” high) holder is the size of a small dishwashing sponge, and it takes up too much space alongside my sink.  All the holder does is squirt a tiny amount of dishwashing liquid onto the bottom of a sponge when you press the holder down.  It’s easier for me to squirt the dishwashing liquid onto the sponge directly from the bottle.  Stored in a conventional flat soap dish, the sponge is easier to pick up.  Also, there’s some waste of dishwashing liquid when you use the soap dispensing holder.  You have to fill the lower reservoir with dishwashing liquid, and because this is a pump dispenser, some of the liquid will always be left behind.  The soap reservoir can't be cleaned easily, because it is welded to the white part that contains the pump mechanism.  (You can clean the reservoir by filling it full of water instead of soap, but the reservoir is hard to empty.)  Of course, the holder dispenses a measured amount of dishwashing liquid.  You may save on dishwashing liquid if you, or someone in your household, always tends to squirt too much liquid from the bottle.

Be aware that once your foodsaver is out of warranty, you cannot get your foodsaver repaired. I have a professional three that set me back a couple hundred bucks and it stopped vacuuming some months ago. It was out of warranty so I set it on a shlf & figured when I get time.Then bought another, (200 more dollars bye-bye) to use. Got a little time and emailed them about getting it serviced and this was their reply:  Dear JoEllen,  Thank you for contacting FoodSaver, a brand of Jarden Consumer Solutions. I apologize for any inconvenience you have experienced with your Pro II and will be happy to help you.  Unfortunately, our factory no longer repairs vacuum sealers.  Here are some troubleshooting tips to try:  1) Check the upper and lower gaskets of the machine.  If either of these is damaged or dirty, the unit will not vacuum properly.  Clean any food materials from the gaskets.  Remove the lower gasket, flip it over, reinsert it into the unit and test the unit.  2) Make sure the open end of the bag is resting entirely inside of the Vacuum Channel.  The edge of the bag should not go beyond the Vacuum Channel. Do not make your own side seams for a FoodSaver Bag. They are manufactured with special side seams sealed all the way to the outer edge.  3) Check the bag for leaks.  Seal the bag with some air in it.  Submerge it in water and apply pressure.  Bubbles indicate a leak.  If the bag has a leak, a new bag is needed.  4) We recommend you pre-freeze meats and other moist foods for 1-2 hours before vacuum sealing. Excess liquids from moist foods can cause the unit not to vacuum and permanent damage can occur.  If these troubleshooting tips are not successful, we would like to help you through additional troubleshooting via phone.  Please have your FoodSaver available when you call. Our toll free number is 1-877-777-8042.  For your convenience we are available 8:00 am to 8:00 pm Monday - Friday, and 9:00 am to 6:00 pm Saturday, EST.  Again, I apologize for any inconvenience.  We appreciate your business and are happy to help if you have any further questions.  Sincerely, Denise Jarden Consumer Solutions [...]  I will not ever purchase another product from them and I suggest you think seriously before you do unless you have money to burn & that doesn't make any difference to you.

10 years ago, we moved into a new home.  At our old home, we had the BEST tap water ever.  There was absolutely no need for us to buy bottled water, our tap water was that good and refreshing.  At our new and current home, we had a different water provider.  Gone was our delicious tap water. It was replaced with awful tasting water.  Our home has a whole home water softener.  But its a waste. It doesn't help at all. Our tap water is still awful. It leaves blue-green rings in the toilet bowl, bathtub and on the dishes back when we still used the dishwasher. (Dishwasher broke, never replaced it as I don't mind doing dishes by hand)  Of course the water company says the water is at an acceptable quality.  Less than two years after we moved into this home, our normally healthy dog got terribly sick and had to be put to sleep . He was in full kidney failure.  He never had any problems before.  We've gone through 3 hot water heaters in the 10 years that we've lived here.  When we moved to this home, we brought our almost 3 year old washing machine along.  It stopped working less than 2 years after moving here.  All of these things could, of course, be coincidence.  But its my belief all of these things are due to our terrible water.  Weeks after we lost our dog, a spring water stand was built close to our home.  From that moment on, we completely stopped using tap water for drinking/cooking and began to use ONLY spring water. (Still had to use tap water to bathe, etc)  And we only give our pets spring water to drink as well.  I was anxious to try the Brita pitcher. I hoped that  it would make our tap water taste better and we'd be able to drink it.  I had no problems whatsoever with the pitcher, it was easy to use.  But we just did NOT notice any kind of new or better taste to our tap water that was in the Brita pitcher. My husband and I think the tap water from the pitcher tastes the same way our tap water has always tasted . There is no improvement.  The Brita pitcher didn't work for us.
Work well for me.

UPDATE 03/10/2018: I initially rated this pan 3 stars. Having had this pan only about 5 months, large swaths of the Teflon are already coming off, and we've never scrubbed it with anything abrasive or used hard utensils in it. This is a new record (for me) for how quickly the non-stick coating comes off a treated pan. Based on this, I am dropping my rating to 1 star - stay far, far away from this pan. It's a poor product that will fail you sooner rather than later.  My original review follows.  ---  I selected this pan for my wife, as she likes to use my cast-iron grill pans but she doesn’t like the clean-up afterward. I will confess that I am no fan of Teflon-coated pans, so I wasn’t too sure about this one. It has turned out to be a decent pan (if you don’t mind Teflon) but there are a couple of negatives that merit attention.  Farberware is not a high-end name in cookware, so I wasn’t expecting this to be a spectacular pan, particularly at the price. I was a bit surprised, though, when I received it – it seems to be very solid with a tightly-attached handle and it is very solidly balanced. This is a big plus, as I have had too many pans that aren’t quite flat on the bottom and as such will wobble on the burner and will heat unevenly. Being aluminum, it heats quickly and because it’s flat it seems to heat evenly. So far, so good.  Some of the other reviewers here have stated that the finish started coming off after the first washing. I have hand-washed this pan several times and so far the finish and the non-stick coating have not shown any signs of coming off. The pan is supposedly dishwasher safe; I have not tried it in the dishwasher so I do not know how well it will hold up in there.  My first issue with this pan is that the ridges aren’t really high enough to make this a proper grill pan. It does leave some grill marks when pre-heated and it does a good job of frying, but it is a poor substitute for most grilling surfaces. Because the ridges are wide and high, it does not grill foods like most other surfaces so the result is something that is more fried than grilled. That said, the ridges are high enough to allow some fats to drain away, so you will end up with moderately lower fat than straight frying, though that depends on how soft and amorphous the food you’re cooking is.  The bottom of the pan is textured and has a neat feel to it, but be careful when running your hand over it. The finer ridges on the bottom aren’t necessarily smooth and I nicked my hand when I ran it across the surface. This was an unpleasant (and unwelcome) surprise. It’s a minor point as most people are not going to make a habit of doing that, but it’s something to remember.  My wife is happy with this pan, though I am not. She has used it several times and it very pleased with how well it works for her and how easy to clean it is. I still prefer my cast iron grill pans and will continue to use them instead, even though cleanup is more work. If the goal is easy cleanup and some fat reduction, this pan is worth a look. If you want a proper grill pan, though, there are better choices available, even in the same general price range.

I purchased the 5.5 qt size model from another source.  I have to wonder about all of the positive reviews and my gullibility believing them.  I had hoped for the taste of deep fried magic, which in retrospect, is unreasonable to hope for without the use of oil. There is no magic in this machine which seems to me to essentially bake foods in a basket, and not well.  The basket on this one is quite large and will hold plenty, so that parts good. But with the food piled deep together, it seems to come  out almost mushy.  I didn't try it for long before returning it to my local retailer, so maybe should have further explored.  But I did do a batch of battered zucchini which came out almost bitter and certainly nothing better than from the oven.. I tried frozen ready made churros.  Came out okay but really nothing special. Think the oven and microwave would have done as well.  Tried chicken nuggets, frozen, not fresh.  Probably my fault on the timing, but came out a bit burned and hard. The air fryer seems to take about the same amount of time as the oven baking. I had somehow thought it was really fast.  Granted, I kind of skimmed the directions on this (I hate reading instruction manuals), enough to learn what buttons to push.  Perhaps I could have learned of more ways to cook with it, and that's on me; however, I was not impressed with the overall performance of the Air Fryer in the way so many others seem to be.  It's kind of nifty looking, something a little different on the counter, but also takes up a fair amount of space with the xl size.

When I take a cold bottle of Crystal Geyser drinking water in my car for a few hours with the air On this summer in Ca.  but leave my car for about 3 hours it’s now warm and yucky. So I thought this Host Freeze bottle would keep my water ice cold! Wrong! I did as direction said , turn it up-side down in the freezer so the gel inside would freeze, I did that for 24 hours then  Took it out , and put a fresh bottle of Crystal Geyser water 16.9 oz,- 550 mL in this Host frozen bottle. Btw it would not take the whole 16.9 oz. !!!! Thinking this host Bottle would be great, Wrong!!! when I got back to my car where this bottle was in between the seats, Covered with sport hat, and covered my front window,  the water was just cool, not even cold.  The idea of this should be accurate but it’s not. It would be Better to buy a good thermos, don’t waste your money on this. I’m just giving this 2 stars!

You get what you pay for.  Sadly in this case, it's even less.  I missed the part that described this a COMPLETELY particle board with a cardboard back. (That's right, I thought it was packing. Nope, the folded cardboard in the packing mix was to be used as the backing of the dresser). The laminate paper is SO thin that if just look at too long it will rub off.  Honestly, If I hadn't had to destroy the box to get it out (SUPER HEAVY) I would have just returned it.  I think the seller is hoping you won't.  Good thing Amazon backs up what they sell.  PROS: *It was packaged about as well as I have ever seen.  The edges were super reinforced.  I've never seen such a well packaged cardboard box. (Having said that, the top piece was still broken in several places  - sigh) *It was packaged securely inside as well. *I am 53, female.  Put it together, by myself, in about 4 hours. *Most of the pre-drilled areas were correcting drilled. *Had all of the screws/parts needed, but see below.. *It's stylish... sort of retro looking to me. *Honestly, it looked like a daunting task.  But once started, it went fairly quickly. *Ording from Amazon.  They (Amazon) are SUPER good at coming up with solutions.  CONS: *Upon taking inventory of all the misc screws and parts, I discovered one of the carpentry screws was broken.  Sadly, the local hardware store did not carry them.  Neither did the local furniture store.  Contacted the manufacturer, NO RETURN CALL. *NO SELLER CUSTOMER SERVICE AT ALL.  Calling the 800 number gets you voice mail that they apparently don't ever pick up or return. *This is such a cheap piece of furniture, it's hard to believe they could sell them for more than a $100. I will use it only temporarily and then it will likely go to the dump. *The directions have NO written instructions.  Pictures are good but did NOT help with the screws that were different than those packed WITH the handles.  Eventually I sorted it out.  But printed instructions would have helped there. *As cheap as this dresser is, I was really disappointed to see that one of the drawer liner laminate was "flawed" right in the center.  I put that drawer on the bottom row, but it is still an eyesore. *The top was broken is several places, three showing on the top piece of the dresser.  Again, I attempted to contact the seller thinking I might be able to purchase or obtain a new top piece.  Seller does NOT RETURN PHONE CALLS OR ANY CONTACT. *Smell.  It smells like the chemicals used in making particle boarding.  I believe there is a study that says those chemicals are harmful. Another reason it will most likely end up in the dump.  If you're still thinking you might try it, here's what you will need to make it easier: A small hammer, a cordless drill (that you can easily control), a flat head and phillips screw driver.  About 4-5 hours, maybe less if you have a second set of hands. Optional: A shot of whiskey for the frustration factor.  Hope this is helpful in some way.  UPDATE:  5/18  Dresser on the way to the dump. Purchased a very nice drawer, on sale, at the local furniture store.  Can't say I'm sad to see it go. I think the lesson here is that in regards to furniture, perhaps it's better to purchase locally so you can actually see and approve of what you're getting. Happy shopping!

I bought this a holiday serving dish.  My sister-in-law loves a cranberry/orange/pineapple jello I make for Thanksgiving and I thought it would be beautiful in the footed bowl.  It was well packed and arrived intact.  The pineapple "carvings" are very smooth.  The measurements are little different than listed, but it's close enough. About 8 1/2" in diameter, about 4" deep.  The foot portion is about 2" tall and the foot base diameter is about 4 1/2". I don't think it looks like an "heirloom" piece, but it is perfect for my jello dish, a small triffle, some tangerines or persimmons. Also to note:  Because it is crystal, it should be handwashed in warm, soapy water and never placed in the dishwasher.  Since I don't plan to use this dish every day, I don't consider that an issue. Hope this info helps!

UPDATED REVIEW BELOW... Purchased two sets and sent to my daughter in Arizona.  She recently moved into a new house and my grandsons now have their own rooms, thus the two sets.  My daughter and son-in-law report that the wood looks very, very high quality and they arrived in good condition. They were very well packed. It did take them the better part of an afternoon to put the first set together, but they feel as though the second set will possibly take a little less time as they are now "experienced" in having already done the first set.  I will include pics when they provide them. I will say that my 5 year old grandson was SOOOOO excited he could hardly contain himself.  My daughter says that it is SO nice to have the drawers and not a ladder.  I also purchased some memory foam mattresses to use with these sets (8" and my daughter reports they fit perfectly in terms of thickness).  We chose these beds because there wasn't any particle board and while I haven't seen them yet in person, I think they will last as long as the boys will want to be in them.  I will update with any information that might be useful.  Further, I do NOT receive compensation of ANY kind for my reviews. UPDATE:  8/2/2016..... I have now seen the beds in person.  And all I can say is WOW.  Honestly.  These beds are the same price as the really flimsy metal beds in local stores here in Arizona... and yet THESE beds are just unbelievable quality FULL wood.  The grain is simply beautiful and rich.  I am SOOOO pleased with my purchase.  I would definitely recommend them.  My daughter reports they arrived in excellent condition via a special shipper. My daughter did say that it helps to have two people to finish the beds.  While one person can easily assemble the stairs, but putting the frame together with the stairs really requires a second person.  She did not feel the need to reinforce the bottom slats as another reviewer stated - but everyone should make their own decision on that. As the boys get bigger she made add slats.  She also feels that they are very high quality and is very, very happy with them.  My grandsons are also VERY happy with them.  I purchased 8" memory foam mattresses (made in the usa) to go on the beds and that really completes the quality.  These beds will last every minute as long as they boys want them and then be good enough to share along in the family.  Again, I do NOT receive compensation of any kind for my reviews.  And I would recommend this particular bed and seller all day long.

Having returned from a cooking trip to Italy last May, I was anxious to start utlizing my recently learned skills. Making desserts and pizza were two of the things that I couldn't wait to try when I got home. I first used the Emile Henry baking stone for pizza and I really loved it. The thin crust I made came out crispy and evenly cooked (475 degrees), my sons loved it. Only complaint I had was that it is not the easiest item to get in and out of the oven (especially when it is fully heated). First time I tried it, I found it heavy and almost impossible to get a good grip on (especially with baking mitts on). Stone also retains heat for a good amount of time (keep that in mind if it is summer and you take it out of the oven to cool). Second time I used it was kind of like a quasi-cookie sheet. I made biscotti which requires to bake the cookies twice (first time in a lengthy roll of almost 12 inches, second as sliced in cookie slices). I only had the oven at 350 this time and the size of the stone was perfect and the cookies came out delicious. I do highly recommend the stone for anyone looking for an extra tool in the kitchen. Cooks evenly, but might be a little awkward in maneuvering it when it is fully heated.

Compared to my [[ASIN:B008LX6OC6 Roomba 650]] (plus [[ASIN:B019OH9898 Braava Jet]]), this ECOVACS DEEBOT M88 ROBOTIC VACUUM CLEANER, DRY AND WET MOP FEATURE WITH SMARTPHONE CONTROL (White) just doesn't cut it.  The robot’s Achilles’ heel is the inadequate battery--a full 4-hour charge gives you about 45 minutes, which is NOT enough to finish vacuuming a small, carpeted living room with obstacles (furniture, clutter). Recharging, to complete the job, takes hours.  Standard suction is also inadequate.  The robot does NOT pick up larger dirt particles, rice grains, or other gritty bits.  To keep a carpet or even a bare floor clean, regular vacuuming with an upright or canister vacuum will still be required.  The robot runs slowly, and the random pattern (required for carpet) changes directions so often, it is impossible to get full floor coverage in a single run.  Cleaning the dirt collecter is very complicated--you must open the top, pull up a handle to remove the dirt bin, open a gate to empty the dirt and dust bunnies, and then clean off a thin sponge that protects a high-efficiency filter. (I had to retrieve the sponge from the trash, after I inadvertently dumped it along with the collected dirt.)  Then you have to reverse the steps to replace the bin.  The robot requires quite a bit of supervision when working.  It has a sloped top edge that causes it to get stuck easily under low furniture or obstacles.  It does NOT climb over flat area rugs, or easily move from bare floor to carpet.  (The instructions say you should use physical barriers, and vacuum carpeted and bare floors separately.)  The sensors could be a lot better--the robot spends a lot of time thinking about what to do after meeting an obstacle, and it isn't very good at detecting stairsteps or “cliffs”.  When the battery needs recharging, the robot slows to a pathetic crawl.  It may not have enough power left to find its way back to the recharging station.  Placing the robot on the charger by hand is fiddly, and the robot is easily jarred off the charger contacts. The robot has an inconveniently placed on/off switch underneath--and you have to turn the robot off if you want it to hold a charge away from the charger.  The robot has three cleaning patterns--random, back and forth, and around the edges of the room.  The default pattern is random, and you must use the remote (included) or the app to change the pattern.  My remote changed the robot to the “edge” pattern, but would NOT change the pattern to “back and forth” (the pattern recommended for bare floors, that I really needed for cleaning under a queen-sized bed).  When it comes to wet-mopping, this robot is too large to mop my bathrooms.  It can't get into the small spaces alongside the toilets, and the round shape means it can't mop close to the room edges or under the cabinet overhangs.  In sum, this Eurovac robot is unsuitable for regularly cleaning rooms that have a fair amount of furniture or clutter, or for wet mopping rooms with tight spaces.  Because my iRobot devices do both tasks easily, I think that this Eurovac device is very expensive (about $450 on Amazon) for what it is.  I wanted a second robot vacuum for convenience in cleaning the upstairs, and was impressed by all of this robot’s advertised features (especially the included remote), but I’m very, very disappointed by the Eurovac robot’s actual performance.

It makes it very clear it doesn’t do that. But why wouldn’t you make it with a grinder in it? That would make this pot PERFECT instead of good. You have to put ground coffee in the hopper. It holds 12 oz. of grounds max. You can skip the hopper if you are making a different kind of coffee that day. But it does not grind. So, good coffee maker. Could have been the perfect coffee maker. Update: After a couple months with this coffee pot, it is now not brewing well at all. It was pretty good at first. Then it started half brewing the grinds. Like, it would dump them, but the water would pool around the edges and not go through the coffee, so you end up with dirty water instead of coffee. And you end up wasting a full pot’s worth of good coffee. I’m not sure why it worked so well for two months and now this. The pot is basically trash now.

This JAMIE OLIVER PIZZA STONE AND SERVING RACK (round earthenware stone,14 inch) consists of a thin, lightweight stone that fits into a simple chrome-plated wire rack.  The rack is rather useless, because it only raises the stone about 3/8” from the surface it rests on, and requires hotpads or a heat mat underneath to protect that surface.  (It's also questionable whether you’d want to serve a pizza at table on the stone taken directly from the oven, because the stone gets really HOT!)  The weight of the stone--very light for earthenware--is a plus.  The 14” size is perfect for baking an 11” frozen pizza and getting a crispy crust.  (My pizza was a frozen Newman’s Own Four-Cheese Pizza.)  However, the stone’s cleaning instructions add up to a huge minus.  To wit:  “It is best to scrape food residue off your stone with a metal spatula after the stone has cooled.  It is recommended not to use water to clean your stone as this may leave moisture in the pores of the stone that may cause the stone to crack when heated in the oven.”  The directions go on to say that if you must use water, you should use ONLY plain water (no detergent), and allow the stone to dry thoroughly before using it again.  The instructions also note that the “pizza stone will acquire stains through use”.  That's absolutely true.  After one use, my stone has burned-on residue and oil stains that make it unattractive for serving anything at all.  (The stone is still perfectly functional for high-temperature baking, of course.)  This product was a disappointment, because I wanted it for serving (with the rack).

This pepper mill is not only for wealthy consumers looking for another way to spend their money. It can be a medical aid in some cases for those with hand problems.  I have no objection to a high-priced automatic pepper mill because those who have hand problems (arthritis, carpel tunnel, one arm or hand, etc.) will find it possible to now grind pepper and coriander without difficulty or pain. After all I purchased a $200 [[ASIN:B000A76VCY Breville 800CPXL Die-Cast Stainless-Steel Motorized Citrus Press]] just to reduce the hand stress I was experiencing with squeezing lemons and limes which I absolutely love and believe it was money well spent.  However, this pepper mill is not in the same league. I expected an elite "machine" that worked simply and well and here's what I got.  In the box is one large pepper mill that is mostly all motor.  It comes with a small container of peppercorns and what looks like a square base on which the pepper mill should rest when not in use.  (NOTE - It slips easily off this base if that is what it was designed for.) There are also 6 AAA batteries included.  The pepper mill has to be disassembled to load the peppercorns and also to extract the motor and load the batteries. Extracting the motor was incredibly cumbersome.  There are 2 sliding levers on the inside plate that have to be shifted toward the inside of the motor with a pencil or file.  In the process, my levers bent and almost broke off (so they are shoddy levers).  Once the levers are no longer holding the motor in place, the unit has to be lightly shaken to dislodge the motor.  It took a while for it to dislodge.  Once the motor is removed, the batteries were easy to insert and the direction of insertion (negative and positive) were clearly marked.  After battery installation, I had to reverse the entire process.  Inserting the motor is tricky as well as I had to line up certain parts, then push down the motor, and re-seat the levers out and under the base lip.  The top of the vessel holding the peppercorns that came with this product also acts also like funnel.  I had to unscrew the top and punch out the center piece then lay this over the peppercorn reservoir to fill it. Once that was done. I screwed the holder to the base. (NOTE - Save the container that came with the peppercorns because it is a measuring cup for refilling the reservoir.)  As per the manual, only black, white, and green (dry) peppercorns of 5 mm (0.2") or less should be used in this mill for optimal performance.  It also grinds pink peppercorns as long as the pink ones are no more than 15% of the peppercorns being ground.  In addition, it grinds dry coriander seeds.  There are six (6) gradations of grind (from fine to coarse) marked on the bottom dial.  Based on my tests (since the instructions are not clear on this), it appears the single horizontal line indicates a fine grind and as the lines increase in number to six (6) lines that indicates a coarser grind.  The grind level is set by turning the dial to line up the desired grind lines with the letter P written on the reservoir (which is difficult to see).  To dispense the pepper, I just lightly touched the "button" on the top of the base to activate the motor. A light comes on and then dispenses the pepper according to the grind level set. The mill grinds quickly and well. Since the unit engages the motor easily, it means if one of my cats touches the top, the pepper will also grind.  In summary, had I paid for this product, I would have sent it back for a refund. The batteries are too difficult to get to, the levers holding the motor in place are poor quality and easily broken, there is no shutoff for the motor once the batteries are installed so the slightest touch of the button grinds pepper, and it does not feel like the elite "machine" I expected. Also, the lifetime warranty is on the motor only and no other part. If this grinder came with USB or other rechargeable and easy to insert batteries, had the ability to turn off the motor, felt like a quality kitchen tool, and the instructions were clearer (five languages follow each instruction) then I would have given this 5  stars.  As it stands now, I consider this a working prototype only and not a kitchen tool that should be sold for such a high price.

Unfortunately, I have just had to throw the ironing board cover in the trash. But a scorch mark appeared recently, and was leaching onto my fabrics when I attempted to iron them.  I am not hard on my irons, nor careless with ironing or the care of my fabrics. I was careful with my use, stick to the appropriate temperatures recommended and I used distilled water in my iron. I do not understand why my cover broke down so quickly. I washed it today (following the tag instructions) to try to remove the brown scorch mark and the fabric of the cover disintegrated around the scorch mark. Apparently to return the cover I'd have to pay return shipping. I'd only get a partial refund as well. What a joke, I guess I'll just have to eat the price and try to find something better at a store near me.
